Replication-initiation report

Architecture: replication_initiation  |  Snapshots: 50  |  Phase progress: 8/9 done, 0 in progress  |  Source: PDF · Markdown summary

Overview

The work is grouped into four Parts, each answering one question: codify what's already there → drive the DnaA-ATP/ADP cycle → bind to the chromosome and gate initiation → add regulatory feedbacks. Phase numbers track implementation order; the table below renders them in narrative order under each Part. Status pills are auto-detected from the codebase (schema fields, molecule_ids entries, process modules, sim_data attributes), so the indicators cannot drift from reality.

#PhaseStatusWhat it does
Part I — Foundations: codify what's already there
P0Region classifier — partition the 307 consensus boxesdoneThe bioinformatic strict-consensus search at `flat/sequence_motifs.tsv` finds ~307 distinct DnaA boxes (TTWTNCACA variants) across the chromosome. Phase 0 adds `REGION_BOUNDARIES_ABS` and `region_for_coord(bp)` to partition them into oriC / dnaA_promoter / datA / DARS1 / DARS2 / `other`. No schema change — region is derived from each box's existing `coordinates` field.
P1Expose the DnaA nucleotide-state poolsdoneWire a listener that emits apo / DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP pool counts every step. Pool dynamics were already running underneath (the equilibrium reactions for both nucleotide-bound forms have been firing in the cache since the start) but were invisible from the trajectory; Phase 1 surfaces them so the rest of the work can be measured against the literature steady-state band.
Part II — Nucleotide-state cycle: drive DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP dynamics
P5RIDA — DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at the replisomedoneAdd a dedicated `RIDA` Step that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP to DnaA-ADP at a rate ∝ active replisome count, and deactivate the DnaA-ADP equilibrium reaction so RIDA's output accumulates instead of being instantly re-dissociated. Hda + Hda-β-clamp complex (CPLX0-10342) and the FBA-level reaction (RXN0-7444) were already in the data; Phase 5 wires the kinetic process and the equilibrium override.
P7DARS reactivation — closes the DnaA cycledoneAdd a dedicated `DARS` Step that releases ADP from DnaA-ADP at a first-order rate, regenerating apo-DnaA. Combined with the still-active `MONOMER0-160_RXN` equilibrium that re-loads apo-DnaA with cellular ATP, this closes the loop opened in Phase 5. Steady-state DnaA-ATP fraction lands inside the literature 30–70% band. Per-locus DARS1 vs DARS2 and IHF/Fis modulation are deferred follow-ups.
Part III — Cytoplasm to chromosome: bind, titrate, gate
P2DnaA-box binding + chromosomal titrationdoneAdd a DnaABoxBinding step that samples per-box equilibrium occupancy each tick from the DnaA-ATP/ADP bulk pools and per-region affinity classes. Emits per-region bound counts (including the ~99 background boxes in `other`, which sequester most of the DnaA pool — the chromosomal titration buffer described in the curated reference). Currently a listener: bound counts are reported but not yet subtracted from the free pool.
P3Replace mass-threshold initiation gatedoneSubstitute `DnaAGatedChromosomeReplication` for the baseline `ChromosomeReplication`. The new gate fires when `DnaA-ATP / n_oriC >= threshold`, matching the structure of the mass-per-oriC gate (per-oriC division gives the same self-limiting feedback) but routed through the DnaA-ATP pool that Phases 5 + 7 set up.
Part IV — Regulatory feedbacks: refine the timing
P4SeqA sequestration window after initiationdoneSeqA the protein (`EG12197-MONOMER`) is already expressed (~1029 copies in the init state); Phase 4 wires its *activity*. The DnaA-gated step records the time of each initiation event and forces its gate ratio to 0 for the next ~10 minutes — modeling SeqA binding to hemimethylated GATC sites at the newly-replicated origin and blocking DnaA rebinding during the window.
P6DDAH — backup DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at datAdoneAdd a DDAH Step that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP at a small constitutive rate, modeling the catalytic effect of the datA-IHF complex described in the curated reference. Pairs with Phase 5 (RIDA): RIDA dominates while replisomes are active, DDAH provides a steady background drain that runs even between replication rounds.
P8dnaA promoter autoregulationpendingtranscript_initiation reads DnaA-ATP/ADP occupancy at the p1/p2 boxes and modulates dnaA transcription rate.

Part I — Foundations: codify what's already there

Before adding any new biology, codify the curated reference and surface the relevant pools. The bioinformatic motif search at flat/sequence_motifs.tsv already finds ~307 strict-consensus DnaA boxes (TTWTNCACA) across the chromosome — far more than the 30 named in the curated PDF. Phase 0 partitions these into the named regulatory regions plus an "other" bucket for the genomic background; Phase 1 wires a listener so the apo / DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP pool counts are visible in the trajectory.

Phase 0 — Region classifier — partition the 307 consensus boxes

done

The bioinformatic strict-consensus search at `flat/sequence_motifs.tsv` finds ~307 distinct DnaA boxes (TTWTNCACA variants) across the chromosome. Phase 0 adds `REGION_BOUNDARIES_ABS` and `region_for_coord(bp)` to partition them into oriC / dnaA_promoter / datA / DARS1 / DARS2 / `other`. No schema change — region is derived from each box's existing `coordinates` field.

Status check: region_for_coord and REGION_BOUNDARIES in molecular_reference

Mechanisms still missing

  • Of ~307 strict-consensus boxes total, only ~8 fall in the five named regulatory regions; the remaining ~99% are in `other` (genomic background). These background boxes are where most DnaA titration happens.
  • Per-box affinity differentiation (R1/R2/R4 vs the named low-affinity oriC sites) is not yet supported — the strict-consensus search picks out high-affinity boxes only. Enriching `motif_coordinates` with named non-consensus boxes is a follow-up.

Before — current model behavior

Today the chromosome carries DnaA boxes from a single global motif set, with no per-region breakdown. Below is the expected per-region count when the classifier is applied to the existing init-state coordinates.

RegionExpected DnaA-box count
oriC11
dnaA promoter7
datA4
DARS13
DARS25

After — Phase 0 (current state)

Coverage: 8/30 named boxes are caught by the strict-consensus motif. The remaining 22 are non-consensus low-affinity sites that Phase 2 must enrich into the box list.

Phase 1 — Expose the DnaA nucleotide-state pools

done

Wire a listener that emits apo / DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP pool counts every step. Pool dynamics were already running underneath (the equilibrium reactions for both nucleotide-bound forms have been firing in the cache since the start) but were invisible from the trajectory; Phase 1 surfaces them so the rest of the work can be measured against the literature steady-state band.

Status check: listener emits all three pool counts; RIDA (Phase 5) and DARS (Phase 7) are wired, so the DnaA-ATP fraction can reach the literature band

Tests (hover for descriptions)

  • ✓ tests/test_dnaA_nucleotide_pool.py4 tests
    test_init_state_has_apo_dnaA_no_bound_forms — Before equilibrium runs, apo-DnaA holds the entire DnaA pool.
    test_equilibrium_partitions_apo_into_nucleotide_bound_forms — One minute of sim is enough for the equilibrium reaction
    test_listener_emits_dnaA_pool_counts — ``listeners.replication_data`` exposes apo / ATP / ADP pool counts

Mechanisms still missing

  • Phase 2 (DnaA-box binding) is wired as a listener-only process today — bound boxes are counted but not yet subtracted from the free DnaA pool. The observed pool counts represent total DnaA, not free DnaA.

Pool drivers — what affects DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP today

ProcessEffect on poolBiological meaning
✓transcription + translation (dnaA gene)∅ → apo-DnaASynthesis of DnaA monomers from the dnaA gene.
✓equilibrium step (MONOMER0-160_RXN)apo-DnaA + ATP ⇌ DnaA-ATPReversible nucleotide binding; mass-action driven.
✓equilibrium step (MONOMER0-4565_RXN)apo-DnaA + ADP ⇌ DnaA-ADPReversible nucleotide binding; mass-action driven.
✓protein degradationDnaA → ∅Routine proteolytic turnover of DnaA monomers.
○RIDA (RXN0-7444 in metabolism)DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADPReplication-coupled hydrolysis of DnaA-ATP, catalyzed by the DNA-loaded β-clamp + Hda complex. Reaction is in flat data and FBA but flux is currently zero.
○DDAH (datA + IHF)DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADPBackup DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at the datA locus, gated by IHF binding. Not yet represented.
○DARS1 / DARS2 reactivationDnaA-ADP → apo-DnaA → DnaA-ATPNucleotide release at the DARS loci (modulated by IHF and Fis at DARS2), regenerating active DnaA-ATP. Not yet represented.
○DnaA-box binding (oriC, dnaA promoter, datA, DARS)DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP ⇌ box-bound DnaASequestration of DnaA on chromosomal binding sites, depleting the cytoplasmic pool. Not yet represented in v2ecoli (DnaA_bound is write-only).

Reference-value provenance

3 of 7 reference values come from outside the curated PDF reference list. Review the flagged rows before relying on them as load-bearing parameters.

QuantityValueSourceCitation + caveat
biological steady-state min0.3in curated PDFHansen FG, Atlung T. The DnaA tale. Front. Microbiol. 9:319 (2018).
Lower edge of the cell-cycle-averaged DnaA-ATP fraction window discussed in the review; specific value is approximate field consensus, re-verify before relying on it as a load-bearing parameter.
biological steady-state max0.7in curated PDFHansen FG, Atlung T. The DnaA tale. Front. Microbiol. 9:319 (2018).
Upper edge of the cell-cycle-averaged window; same caveat as the lower bound.
pre-initiation peak0.85outside curated PDFKurokawa K, Nishida S, Emoto A, Sekimizu K, Katayama T. Replication cycle-coordinated change of the adenine nucleotide-bound forms of DnaA protein in Escherichia coli. EMBO J. 18(23):6642–6652 (1999).
Pre-initiation peak; specific number not verified in the curated reference — Kurokawa et al. 1999 is the frequently-cited primary source but is not in our PDF.
post-RIDA trough0.2outside curated PDFKurokawa K, Nishida S, Emoto A, Sekimizu K, Katayama T. Replication cycle-coordinated change of the adenine nucleotide-bound forms of DnaA protein in Escherichia coli. EMBO J. 18(23):6642–6652 (1999).
Post-initiation trough after RIDA fires; same caveat — primary citation is outside the curated reference.
K_d(ATP) [nM]30in curated PDFSpeck C, Weigel C, Messer W. ATP- and ADP-DnaA protein, a molecular switch in gene regulation. EMBO J. 18(21):6169–6176 (1999).
Order-of-magnitude estimate from Speck 1999. The paper reports both forms binding at low-nM affinity; the specific 30 nM value should be re-verified.
K_d(ADP) [nM]80in curated PDFSpeck C, Weigel C, Messer W. ATP- and ADP-DnaA protein, a molecular switch in gene regulation. EMBO J. 18(21):6169–6176 (1999).
Order-of-magnitude estimate from Speck 1999; ATP form is somewhat tighter than ADP form, exact ratio varies by preparation.
cellular ATP/ADP ratio10outside curated PDFBennett BD, Kimball EH, Gao M, Osterhout R, Van Dien SJ, Rabinowitz JD. Absolute metabolite concentrations and implied enzyme active site occupancy in Escherichia coli. Nat. Chem. Biol. 5(8):593–599 (2009).
Order-of-magnitude cellular ATP/ADP ratio in fast-growing E. coli; primary citation is outside the curated PDF.

Before — baseline (no extras; mass-threshold initiation gate)

Observed final DnaA-ATP fraction 100% sits above the literature band (30%–70%). The DnaA-ATP equilibrium is dominating; RIDA (Phase 5) is the missing kinetic force that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP.

After — replication_initiation (gate + SeqA + DDAH)

Observed final DnaA-ATP fraction 33% sits inside the literature band (30%–70%). RIDA (Phase 5) and DARS (Phase 7) hold the cycle in steady state — the gap Phase 1 was diagnosing is closed.

Part II — Nucleotide-state cycle: drive DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP dynamics

The DnaA nucleotide state is the central control variable. RIDA pulls DnaA-ATP into DnaA-ADP during ongoing replication (coupled to the DNA-loaded β-clamp + Hda complex); DARS pulls DnaA-ADP back into the apo form, which is then re-loaded with cellular ATP via the existing equilibrium reaction. Together they form a closed cycle whose steady-state ATP-fraction lands in the literature-observed 30–70% band.

Phase 5 — RIDA — DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at the replisome

done

Add a dedicated `RIDA` Step that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP to DnaA-ADP at a rate ∝ active replisome count, and deactivate the DnaA-ADP equilibrium reaction so RIDA's output accumulates instead of being instantly re-dissociated. Hda + Hda-β-clamp complex (CPLX0-10342) and the FBA-level reaction (RXN0-7444) were already in the data; Phase 5 wires the kinetic process and the equilibrium override.

Status check: rida.RIDA process wired into the architecture; MONOMER0-4565_RXN equilibrium deactivated so RIDA flux accumulates DnaA-ADP

Tests (hover for descriptions)

  • ✓ tests/test_rida.py5 tests
    test_rida_step_in_cell_state — rida step in cell state
    test_dnaA_adp_equilibrium_deactivated — Without deactivation, mass-action against cellular ADP would
    test_rida_lowers_atp_fraction_into_literature_band — After ~60s of sim, the DnaA-ATP fraction drops from its
    test_rida_flux_listener_emits — The rida listener exposes flux_atp_to_adp and active_replisomes
    test_dnaA_adp_count_grows_with_replication — DnaA-ADP rises monotonically while replisomes are active.

Mechanisms still missing

  • Without DARS (Phase 7), the cycle is open-loop: DnaA-ATP monotonically depletes during a sim. The dip into the literature band is transient.
  • The rate constant `rate_per_replisome_per_s = 0.005` is a first-pass tuning parameter; should be re-verified against measured DnaA-ATP cell-cycle dynamics.

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
New processRIDA Stepv2ecoli/processes/rida.pyA dedicated EcoliStep that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP each tick. Rate = k · n_active_replisomes · [DnaA-ATP] · dt, drawn from a Poisson distribution.
reads: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], unique.active_replisome._entryState, timestep
writes: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], listeners.rida.flux_atp_to_adp, listeners.rida.active_replisomes, listeners.rida.rate_constant
OverrideDeactivate MONOMER0-4565_RXN equilibriumv2ecoli/generate_replication_initiation.pyAfter the baseline document is built, the live `ecoli-equilibrium` step instance has its `stoichMatrix` column for `MONOMER0-4565_RXN` zeroed. The equilibrium SS solver still computes a flux for this reaction but the result is multiplied by an all-zero stoichiometry vector, so molecule counts are not coupled by mass-action.
reads: configs.ecoli-equilibrium.stoichMatrix
writes: ecoli-equilibrium.instance.stoichMatrix, ecoli-equilibrium.instance._deactivated_reactions
ListenerNew rida.* listener fieldsv2ecoli/processes/rida.pyEach tick the RIDA Step emits its hydrolysis flux, the active replisome count it observed, and the configured rate constant.
writes: listeners.rida.flux_atp_to_adp, listeners.rida.active_replisomes, listeners.rida.rate_constant
DataBulk-ID constants for DnaA formsv2ecoli/data/replication_initiation/molecular_reference.py`DNAA_APO_BULK_ID = "PD03831[c]"`, `DNAA_ATP_BULK_ID = "MONOMER0-160[c]"`, `DNAA_ADP_BULK_ID = "MONOMER0-4565[c]"`. Imported by RIDA and the replication_data listener.

Process connections (live bigraph subset)

Is RIDA a constraint on metabolism? No. The FBA reaction RXN0-7444 exists in flat/metabolic_reactions.tsv and is registered with metabolism (stoichiometry, catalyst, base reaction) but it carries no kinetic constraint and no biomass demand, so FBA pushes zero flux through it. The dedicated RIDA Step runs in parallel to metabolism: both Steps read and write the shared bulk store directly, but only RIDA produces the observable DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP flux today. The bigraph below is the live wiring extracted from the composite.

Rendered by bigraph_viz.plot_bigraph from the live replication_initiation composite. Three Steps shown: the new rida, plus ecoli-equilibrium and ecoli-metabolism (which read/write the same DnaA bulk pools).

Before — baseline (no extras; mass-threshold initiation gate)

After — + RIDA (no DARS, no binding; mass gate)

Without DARS (Phase 7) the cycle is open-loop: RIDA monotonically depletes DnaA-ATP, ending the run at 0%. The dip into the literature band is real but transient; Phase 7 closes the cycle by regenerating DnaA-ATP from DnaA-ADP via DARS1/2.

Phase 7 — DARS reactivation — closes the DnaA cycle

done

Add a dedicated `DARS` Step that releases ADP from DnaA-ADP at a first-order rate, regenerating apo-DnaA. Combined with the still-active `MONOMER0-160_RXN` equilibrium that re-loads apo-DnaA with cellular ATP, this closes the loop opened in Phase 5. Steady-state DnaA-ATP fraction lands inside the literature 30–70% band. Per-locus DARS1 vs DARS2 and IHF/Fis modulation are deferred follow-ups.

Status check: dars.DARS process wired into the architecture; closes the DnaA-ATP/ADP cycle with RIDA (Phase 5)

Tests (hover for descriptions)

  • ✓ tests/test_dars.py4 tests
    test_dars_step_in_cell_state — dars step in cell state
    test_atp_fraction_stabilizes_inside_literature_band — After 5 minutes the cycle has closed: DnaA-ATP fraction sits
    test_dars_listener_emits — dars listener emits
    test_dnaA_adp_does_not_monotonically_grow — Without DARS, DnaA-ADP rises monotonically and DnaA-ATP depletes

Mechanisms still missing

  • DARS1 and DARS2 are not yet differentiated — a single rate constant covers both loci.
  • IHF and Fis modulation of DARS2 (cell-cycle gating) is not yet wired; the rate is constant in time.
  • DARS region coordinates are not loaded into motif_coordinates as their own motif type — the existing EcoCyc dna_sites entries are recognized only via the region classifier from Phase 0.

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
New processDARS Stepv2ecoli/processes/dars.pyFirst-order release of ADP from DnaA-ADP, regenerating apo-DnaA. Rate = k_dars · [DnaA-ADP] · dt, Poisson-drawn. The freed apo-DnaA is then reloaded with ATP by the still-active MONOMER0-160_RXN equilibrium, completing the cycle.
reads: bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], bulk[PD03831[c]], timestep
writes: bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], bulk[PD03831[c]], listeners.dars.flux_adp_to_apo, listeners.dars.rate_constant
ListenerNew dars.* listener fieldsv2ecoli/processes/dars.pyPer-tick DARS reactivation flux + configured rate constant.
writes: listeners.dars.flux_adp_to_apo, listeners.dars.rate_constant

How DARS closes the cycle

How DARS closes the cycle. RIDA (Phase 5) drove DnaA-ATP → DnaA-ADP, but with no reverse path the ATP form depleted to zero. DARS adds the reverse path: DnaA-ADP → apo-DnaA → DnaA-ATP (the apo-to-ATP step is handled by the still-active MONOMER0-160_RXN equilibrium). At steady state the DARS flux balances the RIDA flux and the ATP fraction stabilizes inside the literature band. The live bigraph (which now includes dars) is rendered once in Phase 5 → Process connections.

Before — + RIDA (no DARS, no binding; mass gate)

Phase 5 alone (RIDA without DARS) drains DnaA-ATP toward zero over the cell cycle because there is no reverse path. The "After" panel shows what DARS does — flux from DnaA-ADP back into apo-DnaA, then ATP-loading via the still-active equilibrium reaction.

After — + RIDA + DARS (no binding; mass gate)

Cycle closed. Final DnaA-ATP fraction 33%. The two fluxes balance at steady state, holding the ATP-fraction inside the band — the biology that Phase 1 was diagnosing as missing.

Part III — Cytoplasm to chromosome: bind, titrate, gate

DnaA acts on initiation by binding to chromosomal sites at oriC. Phase 2 wires an equilibrium-occupancy binding model over the ~307 consensus boxes; the genomic-background boxes ("other") sequester the bulk of the DnaA pool (titration). Phase 3 replaces the baseline mass-threshold initiation gate with a DnaA-ATP-per-oriC gate — same per-oriC self-limiting feedback as the mass gate, but now driven by the DnaA biology that Part II sets up. Note: the binding listener counts bound boxes today but does not yet decrement the free DnaA pool — the titration force is visible only in the listener, not in downstream dynamics. Adding that decrement is a follow-up.

Phase 2 — DnaA-box binding + chromosomal titration

done

Add a DnaABoxBinding step that samples per-box equilibrium occupancy each tick from the DnaA-ATP/ADP bulk pools and per-region affinity classes. Emits per-region bound counts (including the ~99 background boxes in `other`, which sequester most of the DnaA pool — the chromosomal titration buffer described in the curated reference). Currently a listener: bound counts are reported but not yet subtracted from the free pool.

Status check: DnaABoxBinding step wired into the architecture; per-region bound counts emitted in listeners.dnaA_binding

Tests (hover for descriptions)

  • ✓ tests/test_dnaA_binding.py6 tests
    test_binding_step_in_cell_state — binding step in cell state
    test_listener_emits_per_region_counts — After the binding step has fired once, the listener exposes
    test_listener_reports_nonzero_bound_count — Phase 2 emits a per-tick equilibrium-occupancy report on the
    test_high_affinity_regions_are_saturated — At ~166 nM [DnaA-ATP] (typical cellular DnaA pool), high-
    test_low_affinity_other_boxes_partially_bound — Boxes outside named regions fall back to low-affinity
    test_per_region_counts_match_phase0_baseline — The per-region bound counts at saturation should agree with

Mechanisms still missing

  • <strong>Titration is not yet wired into the dynamics.</strong> The listener counts ~250+ bound boxes at steady state but does not decrement the free DnaA-ATP / DnaA-ADP pool. Phases 3, 5, 7 see the un-buffered total pool, which inflates the available DnaA-ATP for initiation by ~5–10×. The follow-up is to move bound counts into the bulk delta each tick.
  • No write-back to the `DnaA_bound` field on the unique-molecule store — the set-update / add-delete conflict with chromosome_structure is left as a follow-up.
  • All named regions currently use the high-affinity rule. Per-box affinity differentiation (R1/R2/R4 vs named low-affinity oriC sites) and context-dependent binding (variants near high-affinity clusters acting as high-affinity sites via cooperativity) are follow-ups.

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

Why a listener-only process? The unique-array set update mode requires the new value array to match the active-box count exactly. chromosome_structure adds and deletes DnaA_box entries during fork passage in the same tick that binding samples occupancy, so the apply-time count differs from the sample-time count and numpy raises a size mismatch. The listener pattern dodges that conflict — Phase 3 (initiation gating) and the report can both read the listener directly.

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
New processDnaABoxBinding Stepv2ecoli/processes/dnaA_box_binding.pyListener-only process: per active DnaA box, samples bound/unbound from the equilibrium occupancy p_bound = [DnaA] / (Kd + [DnaA]). Per-region Kd and nucleotide preference come from the curated reference. Does *not* write back to DnaA_bound on the unique store (the set update mode conflicts with chromosome_structure's add/delete on the same tick); Phase 3 reads the listener counts directly.
reads: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], unique.DnaA_box.coordinates, unique.DnaA_box._entryState
writes: listeners.dnaA_binding.total_bound, listeners.dnaA_binding.total_active, listeners.dnaA_binding.fraction_bound, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_<region>
DataREGION_BINDING_RULES + DEFAULT_REGION_BINDING_RULEv2ecoli/data/replication_initiation/molecular_reference.pyPer-region (affinity_class, binds_atp, binds_adp) tuples driving Phase 2's occupancy formula. All named regions currently use the high-affinity class (Kd ~1 nM); the default rule for unnamed regions is low-affinity ATP-preferential (Kd ~100 nM).
ListenerNew listeners.dnaA_binding.* fieldsv2ecoli/processes/dnaA_box_binding.pytotal_bound / total_active / fraction_bound plus bound_oric / bound_dnaA_promoter / bound_datA / bound_DARS1 / bound_DARS2 / bound_other.
writes: listeners.dnaA_binding.total_bound, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_oric, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_dnaA_promoter, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_datA, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_DARS1, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_DARS2, listeners.dnaA_binding.bound_other

Before — + RIDA + DARS (no binding; mass gate)

After — + RIDA + DARS + binding (mass gate still active)

High-affinity regions (oriC / dnaA promoter / DARS1 / DARS2 — Kd ~ 1 nM) saturate at 100% occupancy. "Other" boxes (the bulk of the strict-consensus motif hits across the genome, falling back to a low-affinity Kd ~ 100 nM rule) sit at ~50–70% occupancy depending on the cellular [DnaA-ATP].

Phase 3 — Replace mass-threshold initiation gate

done

Substitute `DnaAGatedChromosomeReplication` for the baseline `ChromosomeReplication`. The new gate fires when `DnaA-ATP / n_oriC >= threshold`, matching the structure of the mass-per-oriC gate (per-oriC division gives the same self-limiting feedback) but routed through the DnaA-ATP pool that Phases 5 + 7 set up.

Status check: DnaAGatedChromosomeReplication subclass swapped in; initiation gates on DnaA-ATP-per-oriC instead of cell-mass-per-oriC

Tests (hover for descriptions)

  • ✓ tests/test_initiation_dnaA_gate.py5 tests
    test_dnaA_gated_subclass_is_in_cell_state — The architecture replaces the baseline ChromosomeReplication
    test_critical_mass_per_oric_listener_is_dnaA_ratio — The base class's `critical_mass_per_oriC` listener field still
    test_initiation_fires_at_least_once — Over a 30-minute window the cell should fire at least one
    test_initiation_self_limits — After firing once, the DnaA-ATP-per-oriC value should drop
    test_disabling_gate_falls_back_to_baseline_class — ``enable_dnaA_gated_initiation=False`` skips the swap; the

Mechanisms still missing

  • The threshold (60 DnaA-ATP / oriC) is calibrated against the un-buffered DnaA pool. Once Phase 2 wires titration so the gate sees the *free* pool only, this number will need to come down by an order of magnitude.
  • A more refined gate would read the binding listener directly for the R1/R2/R4 high-affinity occupancy + the low-affinity DnaA-ATP filament configuration the curated reference describes, instead of the bulk-count proxy.

Before — + RIDA + DARS + binding (mass gate still active)

Observed 1 initiation event(s) over 42 min of sim. Each red dashed line marks one (oriC count steps up). The gate ratio in the lower panel crosses 1.0 at each event and drops back below afterward — the per-oriC denominator provides the self-limiting feedback that both gates share.

After — DnaA-gated initiation (no SeqA sequestration)

Observed 0 initiation event(s) over 40 min of sim. Each red dashed line marks one (oriC count steps up). The gate ratio in the lower panel crosses 1.0 at each event and drops back below afterward — the per-oriC denominator provides the self-limiting feedback that both gates share.

Part IV — Regulatory feedbacks: refine the timing

Three regulatory layers refine the cell-cycle timing: SeqA sequesters newly-replicated origins for ~10 min (blocking premature reinitiation), DDAH provides backup DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at the datA locus (with IHF as the gating cofactor), and the dnaA gene autoregulates its own expression via DnaA binding at the boxes between p1 and p2. None of these are wired yet.

Phase 4 — SeqA sequestration window after initiation

done

SeqA the protein (`EG12197-MONOMER`) is already expressed (~1029 copies in the init state); Phase 4 wires its *activity*. The DnaA-gated step records the time of each initiation event and forces its gate ratio to 0 for the next ~10 minutes — modeling SeqA binding to hemimethylated GATC sites at the newly-replicated origin and blocking DnaA rebinding during the window.

Status check: SeqA refractory window wired into the DnaA-gated chromosome-replication step (default 600s)

Tests (hover for descriptions)

  • ✓ tests/test_seqA_sequestration.py5 tests
    test_seqA_protein_already_expressed — Sanity check — the SeqA protein is already in the bulk pool
    test_sequestration_window_set_when_flag_on — sequestration window set when flag on
    test_gate_closed_during_sequestration_window — After the t=0 initiation event, the gate ratio should be 0
    test_gate_resumes_after_window_expires — At t=900s (300s past the 600s window), the gate ratio must
    test_disabling_seqA_keeps_gate_open — ``enable_seqA_sequestration=False`` skips the sequestration

Mechanisms still missing

  • No hemimethylated-GATC tracking on the oriC unique molecule — the refractory window is a coarser proxy for the SeqA-binding biology. Adding a hemimethylation field would conflict with chromosome_structure's in-tick add/delete (same set-update issue as Phase 2).
  • No stoichiometric coupling to bulk SeqA. The window is a fixed-duration cap; depleted SeqA does not shorten it. A future refinement would scale the window by the bulk SeqA count.

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

Why the refractory window approach? Modeling hemimethylated-GATC tracking + SeqA-multimer binding on the unique-molecule store would conflict with chromosome_structure's in-tick add/delete (same set-update issue we hit in Phase 2). The refractory window captures the biology that matters most for cell-cycle timing — SeqA blocks rebinding for a fixed window after each initiation — without requiring a hemimethylation field on the oriC unique molecule. Future refinement: read the bulk SeqA count and decrement / reset the window if SeqA is depleted.

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
OverrideSeqA refractory window in DnaAGatedChromosomeReplicationv2ecoli/processes/chromosome_replication_dnaA_gated.pyThe DnaA-gated subclass tracks the previous tick's oriC count; when it jumps, an initiation just fired and global_time is recorded. While (global_time - last_init) < seqA_sequestration_window_s (default 600s), `_compute_dnaA_gate` returns 0 — the gate is shut. Models SeqA binding to hemimethylated GATC sites at the newly-replicated origin.
reads: states.oriCs._entryState, states.global_time
writes: self._previous_n_oric, self._last_initiation_time_s
DataSeqA already in bulk; no protein addition neededv2ecoli/processes/parca/reconstruction/ecoli/flat/proteins.tsvSeqA monomer (`EG12197-MONOMER`) is already expressed by the baseline transcription/translation pipeline at ~1029 copies in the init state. Phase 4 wires its *activity* downstream of the gate — a future refinement would consume SeqA stoichiometrically (one bound multimer per sequestered origin) so SeqA scarcity could shorten the window.
Overrideenable_seqA_sequestration flag (default True)v2ecoli/generate_replication_initiation.pybuild_replication_initiation_document gains `enable_seqA_sequestration` (default True). When True, `_swap_in_dnaA_gated_chromosome_replication` instantiates the subclass with `seqA_sequestration_window_s=600.0`. When False, window=0 — gate has no refractory.

Before — DnaA-gated initiation (no SeqA sequestration)

Without SeqA, the gate signal is just the DnaA-ATP-per-oriC ratio at every tick. Reinitiation timing is set entirely by the DnaA pool dynamics — there is no refractory window after a firing event.

After — gate + SeqA, no DDAH backup hydrolysis

After the initiation event, the gate is forced to 0 for the SeqA sequestration window (~10 min, shaded amber). Once the window expires, the gate resumes reading the actual DnaA-ATP-per-oriC ratio — and because RIDA+DARS dynamics keep the per-oriC value below 1.0, no immediate re-initiation fires.

Phase 6 — DDAH — backup DnaA-ATP hydrolysis at datA

done

Add a DDAH Step that hydrolyzes DnaA-ATP at a small constitutive rate, modeling the catalytic effect of the datA-IHF complex described in the curated reference. Pairs with Phase 5 (RIDA): RIDA dominates while replisomes are active, DDAH provides a steady background drain that runs even between replication rounds.

Status check: DDAH process spliced into the architecture; constitutive first-order DnaA-ATP hydrolysis runs alongside RIDA

Tests (hover for descriptions)

  • ✓ tests/test_ddah.py4 tests
    test_ddah_step_in_cell_state — ddah step in cell state
    test_ddah_listener_emits_rate_constant — ddah listener emits rate constant
    test_ddah_produces_nonzero_cumulative_flux — Over ~20 minutes, DDAH should have hydrolyzed at least a few
    test_disabling_ddah_removes_step — ``enable_ddah=False`` skips the splice.

Mechanisms still missing

  • datA region coordinates not yet in `motif_coordinates` — the strict-consensus motif search finds 0 boxes in the datA window. Per-box occupancy is therefore not used; DDAH fires at a constant rate independent of datA box count.
  • IHF heterodimer count is not yet read by DDAH. The biology gates DDAH on IHF binding at the datA IBS; we approximate with a constant rate instead.
  • Rate constant (`rate_per_s = 0.0005`) is a first-pass tune. With it, the ATP fraction can dip slightly below the lower edge of the literature band — would need recalibration once the IHF gating is wired.

Architecture changes — what this phase adds, modifies, overrides

What's deferred for the first cut: DDAH currently fires constitutively — it does not gate on IHF binding at datA, and the bioinformatic strict-consensus search finds 0 boxes in the datA window so per-box occupancy is not used. Adding datA region coordinates to motif_coordinates and gating on the IHF heterodimer count are the natural follow-ups.

KindSummaryFileDetail / wiring
New processDDAH Stepv2ecoli/processes/ddah.pyA constitutive first-order DnaA-ATP -> DnaA-ADP hydrolyzer modeling the catalytic effect of the datA-IHF complex. Rate = k_ddah * dnaA_atp_count, Poisson-drawn each tick. Default rate is ~10x slower than RIDA per replisome so DDAH stays a backup.
reads: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], timestep
writes: bulk[MONOMER0-160[c]], bulk[MONOMER0-4565[c]], listeners.ddah.flux_atp_to_adp, listeners.ddah.rate_constant
ListenerNew listeners.ddah.* fieldsv2ecoli/processes/ddah.pyPer-tick DDAH hydrolysis flux + configured rate constant.
writes: listeners.ddah.flux_atp_to_adp, listeners.ddah.rate_constant
Overrideenable_ddah flag (default True)v2ecoli/generate_replication_initiation.pybuild_replication_initiation_document gains `enable_ddah` (default True). When True, `_splice_ddah` adds the step to flow_order alongside RIDA / DARS / box binding.

Before — gate + SeqA, no DDAH backup hydrolysis

Without DDAH, RIDA is the only DnaA-ATP hydrolyzer. The ATP fraction sits closer to the upper edge of the literature band because nothing pulls it down between replication pulses.

After — replication_initiation (gate + SeqA + DDAH)

Over this trajectory, RIDA contributed 56 hydrolysis events vs DDAH's 2. RIDA dominates while replisomes are active; DDAH provides a steady background drain that runs even between replication rounds.

Phase 8 — dnaA promoter autoregulation

pending

transcript_initiation reads DnaA-ATP/ADP occupancy at the p1/p2 boxes and modulates dnaA transcription rate.

Status check: no DnaA-occupancy hook in transcript_initiation

Tests (hover for descriptions)

  • ○ tests/test_dnaA_autoregulation.pyPlanned
    dnaA mRNA level decreases when DnaA-ATP occupancy at p1/p2 boxes is elevated and recovers when the DnaA pool drops; p2/p1 promoter activity ratio ≈ 3 at low DnaA occupancy.

Mechanisms still missing

  • dnaA gene is transcribed via generic `transcript_initiation`; no DnaA-occupancy feedback on its own promoter.

Before — current model behavior

dnaA transcription rate is constant (no occupancy feedback). After Phase 8, this panel plots dnaA mRNA against DnaA-ATP/ADP occupancy at p1/p2 — the closed feedback loop.

After — Phase 8 lands

After Phase 8: anti-correlated dnaA mRNA and p1/p2 occupancy traces; p2/p1 ratio panel.

oriC

462 bp, 11 DnaA boxes, 2 IHF sites. High-affinity boxes bind both DnaA-ATP and DnaA-ADP (Kd ~1 nM). Low-affinity boxes prefer DnaA-ATP and bind cooperatively (Kd > 100 nM). Ordered DnaA-ATP loading on the right arm: C1 → I3 → C2 → C3.

BoxClassSequenceNucleotide preference
R1highTTATCCACAboth
R2highTTATACACAboth
R4highTTATCCACAboth
R5Mlow—atp
tau2low—atp
I1low—atp
I2low—atp
I3low—atp
C1low—atp
C2low—atp
C3low—atp

dnaA promoter

448 bp; p2 ≈ 3× p1; ~80 bp separation.

BoxClassSequenceNucleotide preference
box1highTTATCCACAboth
box2high—both
box3very_low—unspecified
box4low—atp
boxalow—unspecified
boxblow—atp
boxclow—atp

datA

363 bp at 94.7 min  |  DnaA boxes: 4  |  essential: box2, box3, box7  |  stimulatory: box4  |  IHF sites: 1

DARS1 / DARS2

LocusbpCore boxesExtra boxesIHF sitesFis sitesDominant
DARS1632I, II, III—00no
DARS2737I, II, IIIIV, V12yes

SeqA / RIDA

SeqA sequestration window: 10 min (~0.33 of doubling time at rapid growth). SeqA binds hemimethylated GATC sites (>10 per origin).

RIDA: DnaN (β-clamp) + ADP-Hda (N-terminal clamp-binding motif) catalyzes DnaA-ATP -> DnaA-ADP.

Consensus motifs

Consensus: TTWTNCACA (W = A|T, N = any). Highest-affinity 9-mer: TTATCCACA (Kd ~1 nM).

Trajectory under the new architecture

Ran (or reused cached) single-cell sims for three cumulative configurations: baseline → +RIDA → +DARS (full). Each up to 3600s.

Key references

  • Olivi et al. Nat. Commun. 16 (2025): 7813.
  • Riber et al. Front. Mol. Biosci. 3 (2016): 29.
  • Hansen & Atlung. Front. Microbiol. 9 (2018): 319.
  • Fujimitsu, Senriuchi, Katayama. Genes & Dev. 23 (2009): 1221–1233.
  • Kasho et al. Nucleic Acids Res. 42 (2014): 13134–13149.
